page: Vultr vx1-g-64c-256g-3840s is a virtual server instance belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz. The configuration delivers 64 vCPUs and 64 physical cores with a shared CPU allocation. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage consists of a 3840 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU hardware is present.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong compute capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score of 3701.09 places it in the top tier (top 10%) of tested hardware. Multi-core performance under stress-ng reaches 117951.61, which is categorized as strong (top 25%). The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 vector extensions (AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and a high-capacity local NVMe SSD, this instance offers a resource-dense profile suitable for general-purpose and storage-heavy workloads. The shared CPU allocation represents a potential tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, isolated CPU performance, but it enhances qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded applications that can tolerate shared resources. This server is well suited for large-scale web serving, database hosting, container orchestration, and parallelized data processing tasks.
description: Vultr vx1-g-64c-256g-3840s is a virtual server in the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 64 vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM on an x86_64 AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz. This shared-CPU instance includes 3840 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and one public IPv4 address. In performance testing, it achieves a top-tier stress-ng single-core score of 3701.09 and a strong multi-core score of 117951.61. Supported instruction sets include AVX-512 and cryptographic extensions. With a balanced 1:4 memory-to-vCPU ratio and high-capacity local storage, this server is designed for general-purpose workloads, database hosting, and multi-threaded applications.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-64c-256g-3840s is a VX1 Cloud Compute instance featuring 64 AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a 3840 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core and strong multi-core performance.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-64c-256g-3840s is a 64-vCPU AMD EPYC-Turin server with 256 GB RAM and 3840 GB NVMe SSD, built for general-purpose and database workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity shared-CPU instance combining sixty-four AMD EPYC cores with massive local NVMe storage for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 64-core AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz
Equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM and a 3840 GB local NVMe SSD
Top-tier single-core and strong multi-core stress-ng benchmark results
Supports AVX-512 vector extensions and cryptographic acceleration
